my_thoughts: The definitions/hierarchy works: practical = doing (what you can do, on one scale or another), hierarchy (doing > thinking/talking), philosophical = thinking, theoretical = talking, practical = doing. The student requested clarification (seeking foundational definition), which shows they understand: practical needs definition. They'll likely ask about hierarchy next.
accumulated_insight: T3: Student requested clarification ('I should like to understand more about this division. I do not know what is practical')—seeking foundational definition via requests clarification and admits ignorance. They understand: practical needs definition. The comparison_contrast mechanism defines: practical = doing (what you can do, on one scale or another), hierarchy (doing > thinking/talking), philosophical = thinking, theoretical = talking, practical = doing ('It means what you can do—in all senses. Only, 'doing' can be on one scale or another scale. Doing is always more important than thinking or talking. So if we take it that philosophical is thinking, theoretical talking and practical doing, the practical is more important'). Pattern: categorization question → multi-scale refusal → mapping attempt → reversal → definition request → definitions/hierarchy → [next: hierarchy question]. They're grasping: practical = doing, hierarchy (doing > thinking/talking).
visible_response:
It means what you can do—in all senses. Only, 'doing' can be on one
scale or another scale.
Doing is always more important than thinking or
talking. So if we take it that philosophical is thinking, theoretical talking
and practical doing, the practical is more important.
